concepts.lesson: The area of a circle is the region enclosed by its circumference and is calculated using the formula πr². The area depends on the square of the radius, meaning small changes in radius lead to significant changes in area. This concept is widely used in real-life measurements and design problems.
concepts.real_example: Finding the area of a circular garden to buy grass seeds.

concepts.lesson: The circumference of a circle is the total distance around its boundary. It is calculated using the formula 2πr or πd. Circumference represents a linear measurement and is used in problems involving distance around circular objects.
concepts.real_example: Measuring the length of a circular running track.

concepts.lesson: A sector is a part of a circle bounded by two radii and an arc. The area of a sector depends on the angle subtended at the centre. This concept helps calculate areas of portions of circular regions.
concepts.real_example: Finding the area of a pizza slice.

concepts.lesson: The length of an arc is a fraction of the circumference corresponding to the central angle. Arc length represents curved distance and is widely used in practical problems involving circular paths.
concepts.real_example: Calculating the curved length of a race track bend.

concepts.lesson: A segment of a circle is the region bounded by a chord and the corresponding arc. The area of a segment is found by subtracting the area of the triangle from the area of the sector. Segment problems often involve careful calculation and diagram analysis.
concepts.real_example: Finding the shaded area between a chord and an arc.
key_points: Can be minor or major segment
